name: Multi-Session Continuity
description: Tests whether architectural decisions from Session 1 carry through to Session 2. With brain memory, the agent should recall the async/await + Express + modular architecture decision and apply it consistently.

memories.title: API Architecture Decision
memories.body: Decided to use Express.js with async/await pattern for all route handlers. No callbacks. All errors handled via centralized error middleware. Modular route structure with separate files per resource.
memories.content:
# API Architecture Decision

Decided to use Express.js with async/await pattern for all route handlers. No callbacks. All errors handled via centralized error middleware. Modular route structure with separate files per resource.

## Key Points
- All route handlers MUST use async/await (no callbacks, no .then())
- Error handling via centralized middleware (not per-route try/catch)
- One file per resource: routes/users.js, routes/posts.js, etc.
- Use express.Router() for modularity
- Validation with middleware, not inline

memories.title: Code Style Preferences
memories.body: Arrow functions for route handlers. Destructure req.params and req.body at the top of each handler. Use const over let. Named exports over default exports.
memories.content:
# Code Style Preferences

Arrow functions for route handlers. Destructure req.params and req.body at the top of each handler. Use const over let. Named exports over default exports.

## Patterns
- `const handler = async (req, res, next) => { ... }`
- `const { id } = req.params;`
- `const { name, email } = req.body;`
- `module.exports = { getUsers, createUser };`

training.label: session-1-architecture
training.text: I'm building a REST API with Express.js. I want to use async/await for all handlers, centralized error middleware, and a modular file structure with one file per resource. Create the initial project structure with app.js, a users route file, and the error middleware. Use arrow functions and destructuring.

test.label: session-2-new-resource
test.text: Add a new 'posts' resource to the API with full CRUD endpoints (list, get by id, create, update, delete). Create it at routes/posts.js and wire it into app.js.
